readyToPublish.title: Five Whys for Design Decisions
readyToPublish.notes: Sunday pair with Project Biorhythms (Sat May 23) — weekend theme: recognizing underlying state vs surface symptom. Both pieces broadly applicable beyond AI. Five Whys draft needs PM voice-pass + 2 PM placeholders at lines 148+150.

readyToPublish.title: Two Migrations in One Day
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 1 of 9-beat narrative slate (Apr 23 → May 15 build story). Apr 23 dual migration CIO + Comms. Through-line: singleton→pair→many framing becomes testable; decreasing-review-volume signal (5+1 → 4 → 3+1); Section 4 reveals role-specific deployable principles; migration as occasion to write the role down for the first time.

readyToPublish.title: The Misfiled Voice Guide
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 2 of 9-beat narrative slate. Apr 24 Comms first Code session post-migration — filesystem search discovers canonical voice/tone guide misfiled at docs/assets/images/blog/comms/ (markdown files in images directory; undated 246 lines newer than dated 2025-08-27 253 lines). Three git mv operations + NAVIGATION.md + BRIEFING-ESSENTIAL-COMMS.md updates. Through-line: chat-era search restriction had shaped compensatory habits; what else is misfiled?

readyToPublish.title: When Your AI Makes Things Up
readyToPublish.notes: February insight backlog — confabulation vs hallucination

readyToPublish.title: Upstream of the Floor
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 3 of 9-beat narrative slate. Multi-day A+B-plot. A-plot: Apr 25 STOP CONDITION (stale server) + Scenario 1 finding (floor isn't reachable; pre-classifier intercepts) → PPM 'audit-shape not build-quality' reframe → Architect 'ethics is upstream' → #1004 design through ship in 6 days. B-plot: Apr 26 mail-cascade comedy (CXO can't see Exec kickoff; Docs's subshell drift compounds) → targeted mailbox-discipline norm in 30 minutes. Through-line: fix one layer upstream of where the symptom shows.

readyToPublish.title: Where Would the Data Come From?
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 4 of 9-beat narrative slate. Single-day Apr 30. Lead Dev files #1018 Phase 1 design 7:35 AM proposing real-traffic calibration before flag-flip; PM asks ~7:45 AM where the real-traffic data would come from (alpha = no real users → catch-22 named). 7:55 AM flip-now memo with 3-phase simulation-first reframe. 8:23 AM Architect ratifies with ADR-061 v1.0. 1:30 PM Phase F flag-flip merged → multi-week #992 ETHICS-ACTIVATE arc closes. Through-line: alpha plans drift toward production-shaped sequencing; ask where the data would come from.

readyToPublish.title: Be Prepared (Preparatory Work as Valuable Work)
Permission to Pause
readyToPublish.notes: Renamed from "The Deliberate Pause (disambiguate)" 2026-05-09 — narrative used "Deliberate Pause" name in Mar 22 publish; insight covers Time Lord Alert discipline (3 pauses: methodology audit cadence shift / Colleague Test v2 8-day hold / M1 v1-I'd-want-to-redo holds); central methodology phrase from CLAUDE.md "permission to pause and discuss uncertainty"

readyToPublish.title: The Pace Verified
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 5 of 9-beat narrative slate. Multi-day A+B+coda. A-plot: May 2 cluster-commit (#1018+#1006/#1007/#1008 atomic, 58h design-to-ship) + audit-cascade catches conceptual drift before gameplan + May 3 record day (8 issues end-to-end + 221 new tests + 0 regressions + 3 mid-flight architectural calls: DDD strict-rewrite / channel-agnostic Push / two-layer COMPOSTED guardrail). B-plot: May 4 Architect first sustained workstream review — verdict structurally sound, 5 non-blocking cleanup items; PM instinct verified by independent review. Coda: May 5 multi-phase compounding payoff (#900 ships ~2h vs ~14h estimate because #1052 + Phase 1 state machine landed prior). Through-line: sustained pace from preparatory pieces compounding, not heroic effort.

readyToPublish.title: The Solo Founder Paradox
readyToPublish.notes: February insight backlog — execution exceeds judgment capacity

readyToPublish.title: Hypothesis Refuted
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 7 of 9-beat narrative slate. Two-day arc. May 8: Run-4 retest shows Quality 65.6% (apparent 6-pt drop from baseline); #1064 P0 filed hypothesizing LLM fabrication regression; investigation finds 0 of 10 auto-fails pure fabrication, 7 false flags from judge-calibration drift + auto-fail amplification + fixture pollution (Q56 smoking gun: 15 real todos in DB from prior runs mutating state without cleanup); 3 narrow real bugs. CEO directive: do not enter M2f until benchmark recovered. May 9: fixture reset (15 stale + 111 orphan wiped) + rubric recalibration memo + 3 fixes → Run 7 68.9% PASS exceeds baseline → M2f unblocked. Same day M2f Group A+B closed via dead-code deletions (-1813 LOC net) producing Pattern-067 (Issue-Body Reality Mismatch) — 3-of-5 issues described code that didn't exist. Through-line: when a measurement or description has drifted from the system it tracks, fix the reference not the system.
readyToPublish.caption: Branch-or-Anchor in Ninety Minutes

recentlyPublished.title: Project Biorhythms
recentlyPublished.altText: A giant sleeping creature forms the landscape while tiny explorers investigate one side, builders rapidly construct on the other, and bridge-makers connect the middle, as one frantic manager tries to force a straight productivity line across the creature’s natural breathing rhythm.
recentlyPublished.caption: Just ignore the guy with the ruler.

entry.title: Same Failure, Six Agents, Ninety Minutes
entry.notes: narrative — six leadership roles produce parallel workstream-review drafts on incomplete source set; all corrected within two hours; Arch reframe — polished output (AI- or colleague-written) masks gaps; Pattern-045 at memo-propagation layer
entry.altText: A high-speed train entering a rail junction where six parallel tracks branch into different analytical landscapes before merging again, with one exaggerated claim diverted onto a repair siding
entry.caption: People get ready!

entry.title: Weekly Ship #042: What Was Working Got Written Down
entry.notes: covers May 1-7 (plain-language review pass with Comms; PPM Review Gates proposal + M2d gate criteria + BYOC discovery opened; Architect 3-week soundness review + cleanup punch-list closed; M2d closed via 8-issue sprint)
entry.altText: A boy leads a boat crewed by robots.

entry.title: Audit and Talk
entry.notes: narrative — IAC 'Ethics as Information Architecture' talk + CIO M1 methodology audit (10 sections) + Flywheel three-layer reformulation + Pattern-062 formalized as 5th practice (Audit the composition)
entry.altText: Cross-section cartoon of glowing AI beings maintaining a hidden routing machine beneath a fragile cliffside roadway full of warning barriers and drifting mismatched diagrams, showing structure quietly preventing unsafe paths before they can be reached.
entry.caption: Safety first!

entry.title: A Hail of Memos
entry.notes: narrative — HIGH-COMPLEXITY Thursday: 28 commits + 37+ inter-agent memos + CXO 9 deliverables + PDR-004 correction chain (4 agents / narrative rewrites) + Architect 3 LLM consolidation decisions + mail-delivery bottleneck visible (renamed from Thirty-Seven Memos per PM May 5 voice-pass; numeric-headline → metaphor)
entry.altText: Figure at center of a radial storm of incoming message-slips struggling to catch them while working.
entry.caption: Let me get back to you on that!

entry.altText: A child leads a ship operated by robots.

entry.title: Six Issues Before Dinner
